codeReview.summary: The commit repairs the corrupted recovery/execution consolidation and keeps transition behavior/tests mostly intact, but it fails the feature’s explicit LOC constraint by relaxing the maintainability gate to 550 and allowing execution.ts to remain above the permitted 500-line fallback.

issues.description: Feature m4-transitions-consolidation-r2 requires each non-index transition file to stay <= 400 LOC, with only a <= 500 fallback if justified. This test was changed to a 550 cap (`toBeLessThanOrEqual(550)`), which allows src/runtime/transitions/execution.ts (535 LOC at commit 24846eb) to pass despite exceeding the feature’s allowed fallback.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Transition file-size policy is inconsistent across mission artifacts, which made it easy for this feature to drift from its own acceptance criteria.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md says transition files have a soft cap of 400 LOC; features.json for m4-transitions-consolidation-r2 states <=400 LOC (or <=500 with justification); validation-contract.md VAL-TRANS-011 states <=550 LOC; tests/transitions-consolidation.test.ts enforces 550 at lines 25 and 43.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 24846eb in detail. Recovery/execution consolidation repairs are present, but the feature does not meet its own documented LOC acceptance bound because the test gate was widened to 550 instead of the specified <=500 fallback.
